frameworks.name: probabilistic population codes
frameworks.one_line: spike patterns are modeled as samples whose likelihood the circuit estimates

frameworks.name: random sparse connectivity
frameworks.one_line: anatomically motivated random projections support scalable likelihood estimation

frameworks.name: sampling-based representation
frameworks.one_line: intrinsic neural noise is reused as a sampling mechanism for local learning

core_question: Can a biologically realistic, randomly connected circuit estimate the likelihood of high-dimensional spiking patterns at scale?
key_assumptions: real cortical circuits use sparse and random connectivity
intrinsic neural noise can serve as a learning signal
likelihood estimation underlies probabilistic neural computation
manipulations: model architecture inspired by random sparse connectivity
local learning rule exploiting intrinsic circuit noise
slower structural rewiring and pruning of random connections
key_findings: model matches or exceeds current methods on >100-neuron recordings from monkey visual and prefrontal cortex
accurate likelihood estimation from a small number of training samples
structural rewiring further improves efficiency and sparseness of representations
interpretations: random sparse connectivity is a viable design principle for scalable probabilistic neural computation
the approach unifies neuroanatomy, machine learning, and theoretical neuroscience perspectives on neural coding
